_readme: Copy this file to seed-messaging.local.json (gitignored), fill every section with YOUR real messaging, then run: npx tsx --env-file=.env.local scripts/seed-messaging.ts (dry-run) and add --apply to write. See scripts/seed-messaging.ts header for details.

assets.positioning: ONE short paragraph: who you help + the painful status quo you replace. This opens every outbound email body. Example shape: 'Early-stage founders doing founder-led sales lose deals to CRM busywork — logging calls, updating stages, writing follow-ups — instead of selling.'
assets.offer: ONE short paragraph: what Elevay concretely does about that pain, stated as outcomes not features. This is the middle of every outbound body.
assets.cta: ONE sentence: the low-friction ask that closes every outbound email. Example shape: 'Worth a quick 15-min look this week?'

knowledge.title: Pricing
knowledge.content: Your real pricing tiers, per-seat cost, annual vs monthly, pilot terms. Replies will ONLY quote figures that appear here — anything absent gets 'I'll follow up with the exact number'.

knowledge.title: Objection: too expensive
knowledge.content: Your REAL rebuttal, the one you say on calls: reframe, ROI math you actually use, pilot/structure options you are willing to offer.

knowledge.title: Objection: we already have a CRM
knowledge.content: Your real answer: what Elevay does that the incumbent CRM does not, and what stays.

knowledge.title: Objection: security / data
knowledge.content: Where data lives, what is shared with LLM providers, retention, EU hosting — the honest answer you give when asked.

knowledge.title: Capabilities
knowledge.content: The 5-8 things Elevay actually does today (no roadmap): capture every interaction, draft grounded replies, autonomous outbound with approval, deal briefs, meeting prep…

knowledge.title: Competitor positioning
knowledge.content: Per named competitor (Monaco, Lightfield, Claap…): the one honest sentence on where they are strong and where Elevay wins. Replies use this instead of inventing comparisons.
